events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: From the zoo to the stage! At New York City's Central Park Zoo, two male penguins become inseparable. When the pair take turns sitting on an abandoned egg, they successfully hatch baby Tango. Adapted from the pioneering picture book And Tango Makes Three . All families welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.description: Qu’est-ce qu’un kaléidoscope ? Une illusion d’optique ? De la magie ? De l’art ? En fait c’est un peu de tout ça. C’est un instrument développé il y a plus de 200 ans qui utilise la réflexion de la lumière sur des miroirs pour créer des motifs répétitifs envoutants. Dans cet atelier, les enfants feront leur propre kaléidoscope et seront exposés aux termes de réflexion et réfraction de lumière tout en étant captivés par la physique des scintillements des motifs sur les surfaces réfléchissantes. Kids will use recycled cardboard and safe building tools to design unique structures and imaginative creations while exploring creativity and basic engineering concepts. No registration required, tickets will be available.1 hour before program begins. Space is limited.

events.title: Summer Rock Painting
events.description: Get creative and design and decorate your own pet rock. All materials provided.  Ages 4 and up. Registration is required. Please contact 416-394-5320 with any questions.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around  the Branch Tour
events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood walk. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: * WonderPhil Magic Show *
events.description: The amazing WonderPhil performs a kids & family magic show that's packed with laughs, audience participation and incredible tricks that also promotes the enjoyment of books and reading! This program runs for 60 minutes and features the full WonderPhil Magic Show as described, followed by a magic workshop. WonderPhil will teach some tricks that the kids can do at home! Drop-in. Children of all ages are welcome.

events.title: Crochet Club
events.description: Learn to crochet at Eatonville's Crochet Club. All materials are provided and all skill levels are welcome.  Please note there will be no program on July 23rd 2026.

events.title: STEAM Club
events.description: Come explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through hands-on activities and creative projects every Friday. Ages 5 and up with a caregiver.

Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

Age Rating: PG Run Time: 1h 49m For Families. Drop in.

Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 4 to 8 with their parents/caregivers. Registration starts on Tuesday, August 4 at 9 am.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Drop-in each Sunday and explore various arts and crafts, building materials and technologies.

Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Access Employment Workshop: one on one sessions
events.description: Participants will have access to individualized one-to-one employment support sessions. These sessions provide personalized guidance tailored to each participant’s career goals, including resume and cover letter review, job search planning, interview preparation, and referrals to additional employment and community resources. The one-to-one format ensures participants receive targeted coaching and practical next steps to support their employment journey. These workshops are co-sponsored with Acc

events.title: The A(i), B, C’s of Artificial Intelligence
events.description: This week-long workshop series introduces teens to the four "must knows" of Artificial Intelligence. Through video clips, props, an original AI literacy song, discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will explore how AI works, how it’s used, and how to think critically about its impact. By the end of the program, attendees will be better equipped to navigate AI and social media with awareness and creativity. The workshop is led by Keith McDonald, founder and Chief Curator of Toron

events.title: Beginner's Guide to Making Comics
events.description: Have you ever wanted to create your own comic, manga, or anime-inspired story? In this interactive workshop, teens will learn the basics of comic creation through hands-on activities, creative storytelling, and guided drawing exercises. Participants will explore comic and manga techniques including panels, page layouts, speech bubbles, sound effects, character design, and visual storytelling. They’ll also learn how to build engaging characters and create their own one-page comic or manga scene.

events.title: September Acrylic Paint Night
events.description: Join this casual painting session and create your own masterpiece! Theme: Fall Supplies will be provided, including canvas boards, acrylic paints and brushes Please note that this is not an art class. Participants will paint by watching and following a step-by-step tutorial video together. Registration is required. Please register online. Visit or call the branch if you need help with registering. events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Seniors Celebrating Seniors: A Musical Walk Down Memory Lane
events.description: Join Jerry and Peter for an engaging and nostalgic musical experience featuring a rich selection of beloved songs from the 1950s and 1960s. events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
